data.title: Annovis Bio appoints CFO
data.description:
Clinical drug platform company Annovis Bio (NYSE:ANVS) announced on Thursday the appointment of Mark Guerin as CFO.

Guerin has previously served at Onconova Therapeutics (TRAW), now known as Traws Pharma.

data.title: Goldman Sachs vehicle Petershill plans to delist in London
data.description:
Petershill Partners (OTCPK:PHLLF), an investment vehicle created by Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS), on Thursday proposed retreating from the public market as the firm's shares have traded at a deep discount to its book value.

The company is offering to return to public shareholders $4.15 per share, for a total of $921M, to cancel shares of all free-float shareholders. It would then delist the company from the London Stock Exchange.

Petershill (OTCPK:PHLLF), which invests in private equity firms and hedge funds, proposed to fund the capital return with cash, deferred disposal proceeds, and new debt.

Including its interim dividend, the total payment comes to $4.202 per share, a premium of 35% to the previous close's share price, the company said.

data.title: Eli Lilly ends trial of drug to stop muscle wasting with weight loss med Zepbound
data.description:
• Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) has put the brakes on a phase 2a study examining bimagrumab, a drug to prevent muscle wasting, a common issue with quick weight loss, in combination with its obesity drug Zepbound (tirzepatide).
  • A notice on the study's clinicaltrials.gov page states it was ended for "strategic business reasons."
  • The trial was testing the combination of the drugs with bimagrumab alone in lowering body weight in obese patients with type 2 diabetes. However, another trial in obese patients without diabetes is ongoing. 
  • Bimagrumab, a monoclonal antibody, works by inhibiting the activin receptor type-2B protein. It was originally developed by Novartis (NVS) before Lilly took over.
  • A request for comment from Lilly on the rationale behind the trial's termination has yet to be returned to Seeking Alpha.

data.title: Jabil beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 and FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Jabil press release (NYSE:JBL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $3.29 beats by $0.34.
  • Revenue of $8.25B (+18.5% Y/Y) beats by $580M.

  • First Quarter of Fiscal Year 2026 Outlook:

     

    • Net revenue

    $7.7 billion to $8.3 billion vs. consensus of $7.50B

    • U.S. GAAP operating income

    $263 million to $343 million

    • U.S. GAAP diluted earnings per share

    $1.27 to $1.84 per diluted share

    • Core operating income (Non-GAAP)(1)

    $400 million to $460 million

    • Core diluted earnings per share (Non-GAAP)(1)

    $2.47 to $2.87 per diluted share vs. consensus of $2.40

data.title: NuScale Power discloses deal to acquire CFPP reactor materials for $32.3M
data.description:
NuScale Power (NYSE:SMR) disclosed late Wednesday that it entered into a tri-party agreement with the U.S. Department of Energy and CFPP LLC to acquire certain upper reactor pressure vessel long lead materials originally produced to support the Carbon Free Power Project for ~$32.3 million.

If the transaction does not close on or before October 1, CFPP will transfer the assets to the DoE instead of NuScale (NYSE:SMR), according to the agreement.

Two years ago, NuScale (NYSE:SMR) and the Utah Associated Municipal Power Systems terminated the Carbon Free Power Project, which was to be located at the DoE's Idaho National Laboratory and expected to consist of six 77 MW SMRs, generating a total of 462 MW.

Separately, analysts at RBC Capital initiated coverage on NuScale (SMR) with a Sector Perform rating and $35 price target, saying they would like to see more proof of NuScale's partnership model scalability before taking a more positive stance.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: BlackBerry tops FQ2 estimates; initiates FQ3 and raises FY outlook above estimates
data.description: *Other Operating Data Consensus Source: Bloomberg

data.title: IonQ announces record algorithmic qubit score
data.description:
IonQ (NYSE:IONQ), a quantum computing and networking company, announced on Thursday the achievement of a record algorithmic qubit score of #AQ 64.

The company said the score was achieved on an IonQ Tempo system, three months ahead of schedule. IonQ Tempo is the company's fifth-generation quantum computer.

Shares were -3.44% pre-market to $71.32.

#AQ benchmarks measure a quantum system's ability to run quantum algorithms of increasing complexity and size while maintaining high fidelity, the company said in a September 25 statement.

The score of 64 indicates that IonQ Tempo is capable of commercial advantage for certain applications.

data.title: John Wiley & Sons declares $0.355 dividend
data.description:
• John Wiley & Sons (NYSE:WLY) declares $0.355/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.54%
  • Payable Oct. 23; for shareholders of record Oct. 7; ex-div Oct. 7.
  • See WLY Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: DeFi Technologies partners with Stablecorp, backer of QCAD
data.description:
• DeFi Technologies (NASDAQ:DEFT) on Thursday announced a strategic investment in Canada Stablecorp and a commercial collaboration centered on QCAD, Stablecorp's Canadian-dollar stablecoin product.
  • DeFi Technologies joins Coinbase Ventures, Circle Ventures, Side Door Ventures, and other digital finance industry leaders as investors in Stablecorp.
  • Canadian-dollar stablecoin tailwinds: Canadian dollar stablecoins are set to benefit from the Retail Payments Activities Act oversight of payment service providers taking effect on September 8, 2025, alongside growing policy focus from the Bank of Canada and the FCAC. 
  • DeFi Technologies (NASDAQ:DEFT) +1.2% premarket.

data.title: BlackBerry rises after Q2 beat, raises FY26 outlook
data.description:
Shares of BlackBerry (NYSE:BB) rose about 1% premarket on Thursday after second quarter results beat estimates and the company raised its fiscal year 2026 outlook.

The Canadian company's fiscal second quarter, ended Aug. 31, saw non-GAAP EPS of $0.04, while revenue grew about 3% year-over-year to $129.6M. Both top and bottom-line numbers exceeded expectations.

"BlackBerry delivered year-over-year revenue growth and expanded gross margins while reducing operating expenses. This combination ensured strong profitability in the second quarter, beating expectations and achieving a second consecutive quarter of GAAP profitability," said BlackBerry's CEO John Giamatteo.

The company's QNX segment's revenue grew 15.36% year-over-year to $63.1M, while Secure Communications' revenue declined about 9.9% year-over-year to $59.9M.

BlackBerry said $20M was returned to shareholders by buying back about 5 million common shares during the quarter.

Outlook

For the full fiscal year 2026, the company raised its revenue expectations to be between $519 and 541M (midpoint at $530M) versus a consensus of $519.74M, compared to prior estimate range of $508M to $538M (midpoint at $523M).

The company expects non-GAAP basic EPS to be in the range of $0.11 to $0.15 (midpoint at $0.13) versus consensus of $0.09, compared to the prior range of $0.08 to $0.10.

For the fiscal third quarter, BlackBerry expects revenue to be between $132M and $140M (midpoint at $136) versus consensus of $138.04M.

The company anticipates non-GAAP basic EPS to be in the range of $0.02 to $0.04 (midpoint $0.03) versus $0.03 consensus.
